title: The Flawed Logic of Student Loan Forgiveness
subtitle: 免除学生贷款，真的能帮到穷人吗？

paragraphs.en: Some people in the US have asserted that forgiving student loan debt is one way to stimulate the economy and give assistance to those in need. One government proposition is to eliminate $10,000 of debt for 'economically distressed' students. Some in US Congress have gone so far as to suggest forgiving up to $50,000 in debt per student borrower, but does forgiving student debt necessarily correlate to helping the economically disadvantaged?
paragraphs.zh: 美国一些人主张，免除学生贷款债务是刺激经济并援助困难群体的一种途径。政府的一项提议是，为“经济困难”的学生免除一万美元债务。美国国会部分人士甚至建议为每位学生借款人免除高达五万美元的债务，但免除学生债务真的就一定是在帮助经济弱势群体吗？

paragraphs.en: The answer is no. This policy is just giving money away to universities and the most affluent students in attendance. Federal Reserve data reveals that the highest-income 40 percent of households owe approximately 60 percent of outstanding student debt, while the lowest 40 percent owe just under 20 percent.
paragraphs.zh: 答案是否定的。这项政策不过是在把钱拱手送给大学和那些最富裕的在读学生。美联储数据显示，收入最高的40%家庭欠下了约60%的未偿还学生债务，而收入最低的40%家庭所欠债务仅占不到20%。

paragraphs.en: This could be due to a combination of factors: students from high-income households are more likely to go to expensive colleges, less likely to receive financial aid, and more likely to have high incomes post-graduation. Plus, the majority of student debt is held by graduate degree earners, who earn approximately 25 percent more than their undergraduate counterparts.
paragraphs.zh: 这可能是多种因素共同作用的结果：高收入家庭的学生更可能入读昂贵大学，获得助学金的可能性更低，且毕业后更可能获得高收入。此外，大部分学生债务由拥有研究生学位的人所持有，而他们的收入比本科毕业生大约高出25%。

paragraphs.en: Clearly, giving free reign to banks to forgive student debt is a step in the wrong direction. Other proposals for broader, long-term student loan plans have some fundamental problems. One idea is to cancel student debt only for undergraduate degrees and for students making less than $125,000.
paragraphs.zh: 显然，任由银行免除学生债务是朝着错误方向迈出的一步。其他更广泛、长期的学生贷款计划提案也存在一些根本性问题。其中一个想法是，仅取消本科生及年收入低于12.5万美元学生的债务。

paragraphs.en: This attempts to address the fact that Congress' previously mentioned student loan forgiveness plan largely helps out the wealthy, but is an adverse incentive for universities to keep raising tuition and for students to choose to major in low-earning degree programs. Colleges have no reason to make their programs more affordable if they believe students will just take out more debt.
paragraphs.zh: 这一方案试图解决国会前述贷款减免计划主要惠及富人的问题，但它却会对大学持续提高学费、以及学生选择低收入专业构成反向激励。如果大学认为学生反正会借更多债，那它们就没有动力让课程变得更经济实惠。

paragraphs.en: And, students will feel more comfortable making the irresponsible decision to go tens of thousands of dollars in debt to major in impractical or idealistic subjects if they know their loans will be forgiven. This is especially concerning given the pandemic has rendered a college education practically worthless. Students are paying tens of thousands of dollars per year to live at home and be lectured on the Internet.
paragraphs.zh: 而且，如果学生知道贷款终将被免除，他们在做出背负数万美元债务去攻读不切实际或过于理想化专业这一不负责任的决定时，也会更加心安理得。考虑到疫情已使大学教育的价值大打折扣，这一点尤其令人担忧。学生们每年支付数万美元，却只能住在家里上网课。

paragraphs.en: Do we really want to tell colleges that they can get away with providing below-average service for an outrageous cost? In the case of any of these student debt plans, working-class Americans who chose not to or could not afford to go to college will be subsidizing the education of the professional class. Plumbers and retail workers will be paying for the degrees of doctors and lawyers.
paragraphs.zh: 我们真的想让大学知道，即便以高昂离谱的费用提供低于平均水平的服务，它们也能安然无恙吗？无论推行哪种学生债务减免计划，那些选择不上大学或无力负担大学费用的美国工薪阶层，都将实质上在为专业精英阶层的教育买单。水管工和零售工人将替医生和律师支付他们的学位费用。

paragraphs.en: The US government's effort to help those in debt is commendable but is this really the solution that will help the poor financially recover?
paragraphs.zh: 美国政府帮助债务人的努力值得赞许，但这真的是能帮助穷人实现经济复苏的解决之道吗？
